CASE-2026-00042.text: Partial evidence only — proof of address received but income statement still outstanding after 91 days
Applicant mentioned employer has been contacted — delay may be external and outside applicant's direct control
Previous claim REF-55102 referenced — may warrant cross-check to ensure no duplication or overpayment
Case has passed both reminder (28 days) and escalation (56 days) thresholds without resolution

CASE-2026-00091.text: Applicant has held a personal licence for 4 years with no incidents — strong compliance history
Inspection passed with no concerns noted — clean report supports straightforward approval
Festival capacity of 500 — confirm public liability insurance meets the £2 million threshold per POL-LA-001

CASE-2026-00107.text: Two distinct regulatory breaches identified: unqualified staff and record-keeping failures spanning 6 months
Legal team has been copied in — enforcement proceedings may already be under consideration
Partial documentation received on 20 February — staff qualification records were incomplete even before the site visit
Breaches involve both personnel qualifications and administrative processes — may indicate systemic compliance issues

CASE-2026-00133.text: Change from full-time to part-time (22 hours/week) — straightforward trigger with clear evidence
All three evidence documents received and verified promptly (within 14 days of request)
Income below threshold — entitlement increase likely. Check if increase exceeds £50/week requiring team leader sign-off per POL-BR-004

CASE-2026-00158.text: Annual reassessment — routine trigger, no concerns flagged
Evidence request sent 42 days ago — approaching but has now passed the 28-day reminder threshold
Medical certificate requested — may take longer if applicant needs to arrange a GP appointment

CASE-2026-00172.text: Renewal of existing licence with clean 5-year history — low-risk case
No objections received during consultation period — no complicating factors

CASE-2026-00199.text: Routine scheduled check — organisation has clean previous inspection history (18 months ago)
Evidence request sent 29 days ago — the 28-day response window has just elapsed
Evidence sent by both email and recorded post — dual notification provides good audit trail

CASE-2026-00214.text: Evidence outstanding for over 150 days despite two reminder letters — all standard escalation steps exhausted
Telephone number on record appears incorrect — applicant may not be receiving communications
Case has been in escalation since 20 January (87 days) without resolution — may require alternative contact methods or home visit
Change of address was the original trigger — if applicant has moved, registered address for letters may also be outdated

CASE-2026-00231.text: Two objections received — per POL-LA-003, case must be referred to senior caseworker before decision
Both objections relate to noise impact — applicant has already submitted an updated noise management plan in response
Outdoor venue increases noise risk compared to enclosed premises — objections may carry weight

CASE-2026-00248.text: Triggered by anonymous whistleblower report — allegations of inadequate health and safety practices at two sites
Documentation largely compliant but one gap noted: no incident log entries for 6 weeks in autumn 2025
Organisation has been asked for clarification on the gap — response pending
Whistleblower allegations specifically mention two sites — confirm both sites are covered in the documentation received
